name: Silicon Nitride Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Silicon Nitride. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for Silicon Nitride surface cleaning without thermal damage

wavelength.description: Near-IR wavelength optimized for Silicon Nitride absorption characteristics

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for precise cleaning resolution

repetitionRate.description: Optimal repetition rate for efficient cleaning with thermal management

energyDensity.description: Fluence threshold for effective contaminant removal without substrate damage

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse duration for controlled ablation of contaminants

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for uniform cleaning coverage

passCount.description: Recommended number of passes for complete contaminant removal

overlapRatio.description: Optimal beam overlap for uniform cleaning pattern

dwellTime.description: Effective dwell time per cleaning spot

power.description: Typical range for pulsed fiber lasers in industrial cleaning of ceramics like Si3N4 to remove contaminants without thermal damage; starting at 100W for controlled ablation. Max safe ~300-500W to avoid cracking due to low thermal conductivity (~25 W/mK). Source: IPG Photonics fiber laser specs for cleaning applications; academic review in 'Laser Cleaning of Ceramics' (Optics and Lasers in Engineering, 2018).

scan_speed.description: Effective range for 1064 nm cleaning; slower (100-500 mm/s) for heavy contamination (e.g., oxides), faster (2000-5000 mm/s) for light surface prep. Recommended 2000 mm/s balances efficiency and safety on Si3N4's brittle surface. Source: Industry standards from Laserax equipment manuals; case studies in Journal of Laser Applications (2020).

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred for Si3N4 cleaning to minimize heat-affected zone (HAZ <10 μm); picosecond (ps) or femtosecond (fs) for ultra-precision but costlier. 20 ns typical for contaminant removal without substrate melting (Si3N4 mp ~1900°C). Avoid fs for industrial scale. Source: Laser physics in 'Pulsed Laser Cleaning of Ceramics' (Applied Surface Science, 2019).

repetition_rate.description: Typical 20-100 kHz for cleaning; 50 kHz recommended for Si3N4 to achieve fast coverage while limiting heat accumulation (trade-off: higher rates speed up process but risk thermal buildup on low-conductivity material). Source: Academic literature in Optics Express (2017) on pulse train effects in ceramic ablation.

energy_density.description: Fluence range for safe contaminant removal; damage threshold for Si3N4 ~3-5 J/cm² at 1064 nm due to moderate reflectivity (~35%). Recommended 0.5 J/cm² for cleaning without ablation of substrate. Conservative for light contamination. Source: Ablation threshold studies in Journal of Applied Physics (2016); uncertainty noted for specific contaminants.

spot_size.description: Beam diameter range: smaller (50-200 μm) for fine detail on Si3N4 components, larger (500-1000 μm) for broad area prep. 200 μm recommended for uniform cleaning balancing resolution and speed. Source: Equipment specs from Coherent and Trumpf laser systems for industrial cleaning.

pass_count.description: Single pass for light contamination, multi-pass (2-3) for heavy layers on Si3N4 to ensure complete removal without excessive energy input. Recommended 2 passes for industrial surface prep. Source: Industry case studies in 'Laser Surface Treatment of Ceramics' (Materials Science and Engineering A, 2021).

overlap_ratio.description: Typical 30-80% for uniform coverage; 50% recommended for Si3N4 to avoid uneven heating or missed spots while preventing overexposure on heat-sensitive ceramic. Higher overlap for heavy contamination. Source: Process optimization in Laser & Photonics Reviews (2019).
